feedback.text: I am writing to express my wories of my digital video game purchases being destroyed by the seller company or publisher by destroying the video game in question. I would like to know that if I buy a game, then I will still be able to access it in its most basic form even after the publisher had ended official support. I acknowledge that not all features of the game will be fully functional but I want to be assured that the game will not be deleted from my digital library (Example: "The Crew" got deleted from all accounts that owned it, meaning that toy couldnt even launch the game anymore, and you lost access to all features online and offline and for all intents and purposes it was like you never owned the game). Unfortunately this is the reality for most online games coming out these days with some recent example being Concord, Anthem and Need for Speed rivals which will all have their servers shut down and players lose access to what they paid for. I urge lawmakers to add legislations in place to protect consumers from losing access to the product they paid for and for end of life plans to be something that the developers/publishers inform the buyer before he makes any purchases.
